items.title: Timberwolves sign Isaiah Evans to a four-year, $9.3 million deal
items.author: HoopsHype
items.body_excerpt: Timberwolves sign Isaiah Evans to a four-year, $9.3 million deal HoopsHype

items.title: The Minnesota Timberwolves signed Isaiah Evans to a four-year, $9.3 million deal, which includes three fully guaranteed years and a fourth-year team option, league sources told HoopsHype. Evans was the 33rd pick of the 2026 NBA Draft and was an All-ACC Team selection at Duke.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: The Minnesota Timberwolves signed Isaiah Evans to a four-year, $9.3 million deal, which includes three fully guaranteed years and a fourth-year team option, league sources told HoopsHype. Evans was the 33rd pick of the 2026 NBA Draft and was an All-ACC Team selection at Duke.

items.title: Looks like Min/Brk/Chi/Cha Randle deal not yet complete so Brooklyn can use its remaining cap space, if possible, before executing Randle deal. May happen tomorrow, may not. Wolves/Nets can't get Isaiah Evans/Joshua Jefferson on court in Vegas til it's complete.

items.title: GM Matt Lloyd on Isaiah Evans: "We’ve seen him shoot a lot of 3-point shots. We’ve probably watched every single one of them from this season at Duke. We think it’s going to translate positively. We have an analytics model that projects shooting, and that was very positive."
items.author: Jon Krawczynski
items.body_excerpt: GM Matt Lloyd on Isaiah Evans: "We’ve seen him shoot a lot of 3-point shots. We’ve probably watched every single one of them from this season at Duke. We think it’s going to translate positively. We have an analytics model that projects shooting, and that was very positive."

items.title: A couple notes on what the Timberwolves really like with Isaiah Evans:
items.author: Chris Hine
items.body_excerpt:
A couple notes on what the Timberwolves really like with Isaiah Evans: 

Movement shooting, improved on closeouts. They think he can develop into a plus defender as his body fills out given his activity level. Also big fans of his personality.

items.title: Our @johnhollinger.bsky.social gives the Wolves an A for taking Isaiah Evans: "I love this pick for MN. Evans can really shoot and showed strong improvement in his SO season. Teams overthought things on his thin frame. He is by far the best prospect available."
items.author: Jon Krawczynski
items.body_excerpt: Our @johnhollinger.bsky.social gives the Wolves an A for taking Isaiah Evans: "I love this pick for MN. Evans can really shoot and showed strong improvement in his SO season. Teams overthought things on his thin frame. He is by far the best prospect available."

items.title: Isaiah Evans is going to a Timberwolves team that has free agents in Bones Hyland and Jaylen Clark. Minnesota was one of the most frequent users of off ball screens, which should be a good fit for Evans.
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt: Isaiah Evans is going to a Timberwolves team that has free agents in Bones Hyland and Jaylen Clark. Minnesota was one of the most frequent users of off ball screens, which should be a good fit for Evans.

items.title: I love Isaiah Evans for the Wolves! (Nets are trading this pick to the Timberwolves.) Good shooter with size. You can never put too many shooters around Anthony Edwards.

items.title: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets will select Isaiah Evans with the No. 33 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. Evans is headed to the Minnesota Timberwolves as part of a 3-team trade, which also landed Julius Randle in Brooklyn and sent Nic Claxton to the Bulls.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets will select Isaiah Evans with the No. 33 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. Evans is headed to the Minnesota Timberwolves as part of a 3-team trade, which also landed Julius Randle in Brooklyn and sent Nic Claxton to the Bulls.

items.title: Bulls should trade up in the second round tonight. Three targets: Bruce Thornton, Meleek Thomas, and Henri Veesaar. I'd put Isaiah Evans fourth. Bryson inherited a ton of seconds from AK's trade deadline purge. This is what they're for. Go get a third contributor from this draft.
items.author: Ricky O'Donnell
items.body_excerpt: Bulls should trade up in the second round tonight. Three targets: Bruce Thornton, Meleek Thomas, and Henri Veesaar. I'd put Isaiah Evans fourth. Bryson inherited a ton of seconds from AK's trade deadline purge. This is what they're for. Go get a third contributor from this draft.
